title: Anacamptis dulukae
intro: Anacamptis dulukae is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to the island of Corsica, characterized by its unique, white and purple-tinged flowers. Traditionally, the plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. The bioactive constituents of Anacamptis dulukae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In Corsican herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections and digestive issues.
